race.name: Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA
race.display_name: Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA
race.tagline: 85 flat miles of Italian flair in sunny Miami—pure pro vibes for amateurs.

vitals.field_size: Hundreds of recreational riders, amateurs to enthusiasts, snowbirds included
vitals.registration: Includes custom jersey, medallion, bib, expo entry, pasta dinner, post-ride lunch—pure Italian immersion.
vitals.prize_purse: Awards by aggregate timed segment results, no cash purse.
vitals.aid_stations: 5 fully stocked SAG stops on Gran Fondo.
vitals.cutoff_time: Flexible for all levels, no strict cutoff.
distance_options: Gran Fondo: 85mi, 282ft — 4 timed segments, 5 aid stations
Medio: 56mi, minimal — 3 timed segments, 3 aid stations
Piccolo: 35mi, minimal — 2 timed segments, 1 aid station
Ragazzi Fun Ride: 3 short non-competitive distances

climate.description: February in Doral delivers balmy highs of 77°F (25°C) and lows around 65°F (18°C), with scant 2 inches of rain. Expect endless sunshine perfect for ripping open roads. Minimal wind risk seals it as cycling paradise.

terrain.surface: Silky smooth asphalt loops through Miami-Dade suburbs and urban stretches. No cracks, no climbs—just relentless speed on open roads. Paved from start to finish, built for watts over heroism.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: Solid T3 for accessible fun and Giro branding in a flat paradise. Lacks epic suffering or depth but nails logistics, climate, and pro atmosphere. Prestige bump from Italian heritage keeps it punchy above pure beginner fests.

logistics.explanation: MIA airport minutes away, on-site packet pickup and expo at City Place Doral—flawless. Weekend package with pasta dinner and lodging screams pro treatment. Zero excuses for not showing up dialed.

length.explanation: 85 miles hits Gran Fondo sweet spot for full-day suffering without insanity. Four timed segments keep it spicy over pure slog. Perfect for building base or chasing KOMs in flats.

technicality.explanation: Flat paved roads demand zero bike-handling wizardry—just raw power. Urban loops might throw light traffic, but it's toddler-easy technically. Pros yawn, sprinters salivate.

elevation.explanation: Minimal 282ft is a joke for climbers—pure spinner's delight. Florida flatness means no suffering zones, just endless tempo. Elevation junkies look elsewhere.

climate.explanation: 77°F sunny perfection trumps northern slop every time. Low rain risk lets you pin it without weather roulette. Snowbirds' wet dream for winter training.

altitude.explanation: Sea level means zero hypoxia drama. Pure oxygen fest for max watts. High-altitude aces get no edge here.

adventure.explanation: Urban Miami loops lack wild epicness—no remote vistas or gravel surprises. Familiar Florida flats feel like group ride extension. Branded expo adds minor flair.

prestige.explanation: Giro d'Italia stamp and Owen Shott as Grand Marshal scream legitimacy. Global series in 6 countries elevates it beyond local fondo. Amateur pro cosplay at its finest.

race_quality.explanation: Timed segments over full course? Smart innovation for fair racing. Aid stations, swag, and awards nail execution. Feels legit without UCI pretension.

experience.explanation: Italian expo village, pasta feast, custom kit—total immersion. Finisher medallions and pro vibes make you feel like a Maglia Rosa contender. Unbeatable atmosphere.

community.explanation: Amateur-friendly with kids rides and charity ties builds vibe. Snowbird cyclists flock for winter fix. Giro brand unites global fondo nuts.

field_depth.explanation: Recreational masses dominate—no elite peloton showdowns. Fun for all, but shallow competition lacks GC intrigue. Segment leaderboards add slight edge.

value.explanation: $150-165 bags jersey, dinner, expo—steal for branded fun. Way better than faceless fondos. Charity bonus sweetens the pot.

expenses.explanation: Miami flights cheap off-season, on-site lodging smart. No hidden costs beyond entry—budget win. Beats Euro travel hassles.

biased_opinion.summary: This Giro-branded blast delivers pro polish on Florida's flattest canvas—85 miles of pure wattage without a single climb to cry about. Timed segments and pasta parties make it addictively fun for speed demons dodging winter. Prestige punches above its easy terrain, but don't expect suffering glory. Global expansion and Owen Shott's star power cement its buzz. Ideal for base miles with swagger.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Pin it if you crave fast flats and Italian vibes. Skip if hills are your religion. Winter essential for northerners.

final_verdict.one_liner: Blistering flats with Giro glamour—fun but forget the climber's ego.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're a power hunter chasing winter miles or family fondo vibes. Skip if you need climbs or elite battles—too easy for masochists.
final_verdict.alternatives: Unbound Gravel (unbound-gravel) for epic adventure, Hincapie Gran Fondo (hincapie-gran-fondo) for Southeast hills, Velothon (velothon) for Euro prestige.

logistics.airport: Miami International (MIA) just minutes away—easiest access imaginable. Fly in, roll out.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Book City Place Doral hotels for expo, pickup, and pasta dinner synergy. Weekend package seals it.
logistics.food: Saturday pasta blowout fuels rides; post-ride lunch hits right. Expo village overflows Italian eats.
logistics.packet_pickup: On-site Saturday at Race Central—jersey, bib, swag ready.

history.founder: Giro d'Italia organizers expanding pro routes to amateurs
history.origin_story: Born from Giro's pro parcours, Ride Like a Pro hit amateurs in 2023 across 6 countries by 2025. USA debut smashed in St. Augustine January 2025 with champ Owen Shott. Relocated to Doral Miami for 2026's sunnier, Italian-flavored winter escape.
notable_moments: 2025 St. Augustine debut with Owen Shott
history.reputation: Rising star for accessible pro vibes. Charity support and Giro branding build cult following fast. Flat-fast formula hooks speed freaks.

course_description.character: Silky flat loops from Doral through Miami-Dade suburbs scream pure speed. Four timed segments spice endless asphalt with KOM hunts. Minimal elevation lets watts rule—no tactical climbing games. Urban glimpses mix with open-road blasts for pro-peloton feel.

suffering_zones.label: Segment 1 Sprint
suffering_zones.desc: First timed blast awakens legs on fresh flats. Pure power test before aid station relief. Pace yourself or crack early.

suffering_zones.label: Mid-Race Grinder
suffering_zones.desc: Longest segment demands sustained tempo amid urban loops. Wind exposure possible—shelter smart. Legs scream by end.

suffering_zones.label: Late Surge
suffering_zones.desc: Penultimate punch with fatigue mounting. Short but fierce for GC positioning. Final aid fuels the close.

suffering_zones.label: Finish Flyer
suffering_zones.desc: Glory segment to tape—empty tanks optional. Crowd energy at Doral pushes the win. Iconic pro finish.

course_description.signature_challenge: Aggregate timed segments over full 85 miles—winners forged by consistent power, not one big attack. Flats amplify small gaps; strategy trumps suffering.

guide_variables.race_name: Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA
guide_variables.race_terrain: Flat paved roads with timed segments

non_negotiables.why: Builds unbreakable tempo for 85-mile watts fest. Segments punish inconsistency—train steady power. Flats expose weak engines early.
Mimics race's KOM hunts amid fatigue. Sharpens pacing for aggregate scoring. Turns good riders into segment kings.
